4.1 Technical Electives 
This elective requirement gives each student the freedom to develop a technical course of 
study in electrical engineering of considerable breadth and focus. The Advanced Core ECE 
Electives provide an introduction to the major sub-disciplines of electrical engineering, such 
as electrical and computer engineering; bioengineering; acoustics; and magnetic resonance 
engineering; circuits and signal processing; communication and control; computer engineering; 
electromagnetics; optics; and remote sensing; microelectronics and quantum electronics; 
power and energy systems.

unique interest. This freedom plays a critical role in helping students define their research 
specialties. Students are encouraged to take cross-disciplinary courses as free electives. 
Students must complete a minimum of twelve (12) AUs in free electives.

1. Overview 
The Zhejiang University/University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign Institute (ZJU-UIUC Institute, 
ZJUI) offers a joint dual-degree engineering program. Upon successful completion of the 
program, and after meeting the graduation requirements of both universities, students will 
obtain bachelor’s degrees separately from Zhejiang University (ZJU) and the University of 
Illinois Urbana-Champaign (UIUC). 
Electrical engineering is a multifaceted discipline that over the last century has produced an 
astounding progression of technological innovations that have shaped virtually every aspect 
of modern life. Electrical engineers need a broad and solid foundation in mathematics and 
physics to support their education in

life. Electrical engineers need a broad and solid foundation in mathematics and 
physics to support their education in the engineering principles of analysis, synthesis, design, 
implementation, and testing of the devices and systems that provide the bedrock of modern 
energy, communication, sensing, computing, medical, security, and defense infrastructures. 
Within each sub-discipline, there are application domains that strongly rely on hands-on 
experimental work or that are based on theoretical, mathematical, and computational 
approaches. The multidisciplinary nature of electrical engineering education addresses the 
growing demand for the innovation and design of sensing, communication, computing, and 
decision-making systems of increasing complexity in consumer, defense, and medical

nsing, communication, computing, and 
decision-making systems of increasing complexity in consumer, defense, and medical 
applications. 
The core curriculum focuses on fundamental courses on circuits, electromagnetics, solid-state 
electronics, and computer systems, leading to a comprehensive array of specialized courses 
and laboratories in all of the important areas of modern electrical engineering. These range 
from power and energy systems to electronic, optoelectronic, and photonic devices; integrated 
circuits; telecommunications and remote sensing; control systems; robotics; signal processing; 
and biomedical instrumentation and sensing. 
Students are encouraged to take courses and participate in research projects in 
interdisciplinary areas with Civil and Environmental Engineering,

take courses and participate in research projects in 
interdisciplinary areas with Civil and Environmental Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, 
Computer Engineering, and others during their junior and senior years.

ii). Transfer Requirement

non-degree student to degree student in Fall semester of senior year.

4. Elective Courses 
4.1 Technical Electives 
This elective requirement gives each student the freedom to develop a technical course of 
study in electrical engineering of considerable breadth and focus. The Advanced Core ECE 
Electives introduce the major sub-disciplines of electrical engineering, such as electrical and 
computer engineering; bioengineering; acoustics; and magnetic resonance engineering; 
circuits and signal processing; communication and control; computer engineering; 
electromagnetics; optics; and remote sensing; microelectronics and quantum electronics; 
power and energy systems.

4.3 Free Electives 
These unrestricted electives give students the opportunity to explore any intellectual area of 
unique interest. This freedom plays a critical role in helping students define their research 
specialties. Students are encouraged to take cross-disciplinary courses as free electives. 
Students must complete a minimum of twelve (12) AUs in free electives. 
 
ZJU required general education courses cannot be counted as free electives to satisfy with 
the requirements of UIUC degree.

1. Overview 
The Zhejiang University/University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign Institute (ZJU-UIUC Institute) 
offers a joint dual-degree engineering program. Upon successful completion of the program, 
and after meeting the graduation requirements of both universities, students will obtain 
bachelor’s degrees separately from Zhejiang University (ZJU) and the University of Illinois 
Urbana-Champaign (UIUC). 
Mechanical engineering may be the most diverse of the engineering fields, embracing many 
sub-fields and affecting all aspects of our lives. Mechanical engineers work on new machines, 
products, and processes that hold the promise of better lives for all of us. They are concerned 
with both technological and economic aspec

sses that hold the promise of better lives for all of us. They are concerned 
with both technological and economic aspects in the design, development, and use of their 
products. Today, one of the challenges is to design efficient, low-cost machines and processes 
that use the fewest possible natural resources to improve the lives of people throughout the 
world. 
The technical portion of the Mechanical Engineering program curriculum is designed as a 
sequence of increasingly specialized experiences. The student’s first year is spent mastering 
the basics of science: math, chemistry, and physics. Building on this base, in the second year, 
students begin to take fundamental engineering courses such as statics, dynamics, basic 
circuits and electronics, thermodynamics, and strength of mater

al engineering courses such as statics, dynamics, basic 
circuits and electronics, thermodynamics, and strength of materials. By the third year, students 
are taking specialized mechanical engineering courses in the sub-fields of fluid mechanics, 
heat transfer, dynamic systems and controls, materials, mechanical design, and 
manufacturing. Finally, during the senior year, students have the opportunity to both broaden 
and deepen their knowledge of the field through technical elective courses. At the end of the 
curriculum, students take the capstone senior design course in which the knowledge and skills 
they have learned are applied to projects submitted to the department by industrial firms or by 
faculty members. Engineering design, communication, teamwork, and laboratory experiences

nt by industrial firms or by 
faculty members. Engineering design, communication, teamwork, and laboratory experiences 
are integrated throughout the curriculum from the first year to the last year. 
During their junior and senior years students are encouraged to take courses and involve 
themselves in research projects in cross-disciplinary areas with Civil and Environmental 
Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, and others. 
 
2. Graduation Requirement 
2.1 Graduation Requirements for UIUC degree 
2.1.1 
Grade Point Average Requirement 
A student must maintain a minimum GPA of 2.0 (A=4.0) to remain in good standing and 
graduate. UIUC degree is subjected to the final review by UIUC. 
2.1.2 
Junior Eligibility Requirement 
To be eligible to enroll in the ME courses li

ected to the final review by UIUC. 
2.1.2 
Junior Eligibility Requirement 
To be eligible to enroll in the ME courses listed in the third year of the curriculum, a student 
must have completed, with a combined 2.25 grade point average, the mathematics, physics, 
computer science, and electrical and computer engineering courses listed in the first two years.

2.1.4 
Additional requirements  
For UIUC degree, in addition to the specific course and grade point average requirements 
listed above, each candidate for a bachelor’s degree from UIUC must meet the following 
requirements: 
i). Residency Requirement  
Earn a minimum sixty (60) semester AUs of UIUC credit, of which at least twenty-one (21) AUs 
must be 300 or 400 level UIUC credit courses.  
ii). Transfer Requirement  
Students should have a satisfactory English Proficiency Test score on TOEFL, IELT

courses.  
ii). Transfer Requirement  
Students should have a satisfactory English Proficiency Test score on TOEFL, IELTS or other 
exams approved by UIUC, and maintain a good standing on academic studies that all term 
GPAs, overall GPA on UIUC courses are suggested to be above 2.5. In junior year, students

Mechanical Engineering

will apply for international transfer to be admitted to UIUC and change academic status from 
non-degree student to degree student in Fall semester of senior year.  
To remain in good academic standing and to graduate from the ME curriculum, a student must 
have the curriculum courses satisfied with the TGPA or GPA requirements listed in section 8. 
2.2 Graduation Requirements for ZJU degree 
In addition to the 128-AU requirement listed above, the curriculum leading to the degree of 
Bachelor of Engineering from ZJU requires students to complete additional 33.5 credits of 
ZJU-required general education courses for domestic students and additional six courses (22 
credits) in Chinese language and society study for internatio

rses for domestic students and additional six courses (22 
credits) in Chinese language and society study for international students. Please refer to 
section 5 for details.

student and provide preparation for later courses. The science, statistics, and MechSE 
electives, and additional technical courses, stress the rigorous analysis, design, and statistics 
principles practiced in mechanical engineering.

Phys 213 and Phys 214, or MCB 150.

1. Overview 
The Zhejiang University/University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign Institute (ZJU-UIUC Institute) 
offers a joint dual-degree engineering program. Upon successful completion of the program, 
and after meeting the graduation requirements of both universities, students will obtain 
bachelor’s degrees separately from Zhejiang University (ZJU) and the University of Illinois 
Urbana-Champaign (UIUC). 
Mechanical engineering may be the most diverse of the engineering fields, embracing many 
sub-fields and affecting all aspects of our lives. Mechanical engineers work on new machines, 
products, and processes that hold the promise of better lives for all of us. They are concerned 
with both technological and economic aspec

sses that hold the promise of better lives for all of us. They are concerned 
with both technological and economic aspects in the design, development, and use of their 
products. Today, one of the challenges is to design efficient, low-cost machines and processes 
that use the fewest possible natural resources to improve the lives of people throughout the 
world. 
The technical portion of the Mechanical Engineering program curriculum is designed as a 
sequence of increasingly specialized experiences. The student’s first year is spent mastering 
the basics of science: math, chemistry, and physics. Building on this base, in the second year, 
students begin to take fundamental engineering courses such as statics, dynamics, basic 
circuits and electronics, thermodynamics, and strength of mater

al engineering courses such as statics, dynamics, basic 
circuits and electronics, thermodynamics, and strength of materials. By the third year, students 
are taking specialized mechanical engineering courses in the sub-fields of fluid mechanics, 
heat transfer, dynamic systems and controls, materials, mechanical design, and 
manufacturing. Finally, during the senior year, students have the opportunity to both broaden 
and deepen their knowledge of the field through technical elective courses. At the end of the 
curriculum, students take the capstone senior design course in which the knowledge and skills 
they have learned are applied to projects submitted to the department by industrial firms or by 
faculty members. Engineering design, communication, teamwork, and laboratory experiences

nt by industrial firms or by 
faculty members. Engineering design, communication, teamwork, and laboratory experiences 
are integrated throughout the curriculum from the first year to the last year. 
During their junior and senior years students are encouraged to take courses and involve 
themselves in research projects in cross-disciplinary areas with Civil and Environmental 
Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, and others.

requirements:

Earn a minimum sixty (60) semester AUs of UIUC credit, of which at least twenty-one (21) AUs 
must be 300 or 400 level UIUC credit courses.

Students should have a satisfactory English Proficiency Test score on TOEFL, IELTS

ourses.  
ii). Transfer Requirement

exams approved by UIUC, and maintain a good standing on academic studies that all term 
GPAs, overall GPA on UIUC courses are suggested to be above 2.5. In junior year, students

1. Overview 
The Zhejiang University/University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign Institute (ZJU-UIUC Institute, 
ZJUI) offers a joint dual-degree engineering program. Upon successful completion of the 
program, and after meeting the graduation requirements of both universities, students will 
obtain bachelor’s degrees separately from Zhejiang University (ZJU) and the University of 
Illinois Urbana-Champaign (UIUC). 
Mechanical engineering may be the most diverse of the engineering fields, embracing many 
sub-fields and affecting all aspects of our lives. Mechanical engineers work on new machines, 
products, and processes that hold the promise of better lives for all of us. They are concerned 
with both technological and economic

processes that hold the promise of better lives for all of us. They are concerned 
with both technological and economic aspects in the design, development, and use of their 
products. Today, one of the challenges is to design efficient, low-cost machines and processes 
that use the fewest possible natural resources to improve the lives of people throughout the 
world. 
The technical portion of the Mechanical Engineering program curriculum is designed as a 
sequence of increasingly specialized experiences. The student’s first year is spent mastering 
the basics of science: math, chemistry, and physics. Building on this base, in the second year, 
students begin to take fundamental engineering courses such as statics, dynamics, basic 
circuits and electronics, thermodynamics, and strength of

damental engineering courses such as statics, dynamics, basic 
circuits and electronics, thermodynamics, and strength of materials. By the third year, students 
are taking specialized mechanical engineering courses in the sub-fields of fluid mechanics, 
heat transfer, dynamic systems and controls, materials, mechanical design, and 
manufacturing. Finally, during the senior year, students have the opportunity to both broaden 
and deepen their knowledge of the field through technical elective courses. At the end of the 
curriculum, students take the capstone senior design course in which the knowledge and skills 
they have learned are applied to projects submitted to the department by industrial firms or by 
faculty members. Engineering design, communication, teamwork, and laboratory experie

partment by industrial firms or by 
faculty members. Engineering design, communication, teamwork, and laboratory experiences 
are integrated throughout the curriculum from the first year to the last year. 
During their junior and senior years students are encouraged to take courses and involve 
themselves in research projects in cross-disciplinary areas with Civil and Environmental 
Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, and others.

Grade Point Average Requirement 
A student must maintain a minimum GPA of 2.0 (A=4.0) to remain in good standing and 
graduate. UIUC degree is subjected to the final review by UIUC.

To be eligible to enroll in the ME courses listed in the third year of the curriculum, a student 
must have completed, with a combined 2.25 grade point average, the mathematics, physics, 
computer science, and electrical and computer engineering courses listed in the first two years.

principles practiced in mechanical engineering.

1. Overview 
The Zhejiang University/University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign Institute (ZJU-UIUC 
Institute, ZJUI) offers a joint dual-degree engineering program. Upon successful 
completion of the program, and after meeting the graduation requirements of both 
universities, students will obtain bachelor’s degrees separately from Zhejiang University 
(ZJU) and the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign (UIUC). 
Mechanical engineering may be the most diverse of the engineering fields, embracing 
many sub-fields and affecting all aspects of our lives. Mechanical engineers work on new 
machines, products, and processes that hold the promise of better lives for all of us. They 
are concerned with both technological and economic

processes that hold the promise of better lives for all of us. They 
are concerned with both technological and economic aspects in the design, development, 
and use of their products. Today, one of the challenges is to design efficient, low-cost 
machines and processes that use the fewest possible natural resources to improve the 
lives of people throughout the world. 
The technical portion of the Mechanical Engineering program curriculum is designed as a 
sequence of increasingly specialized experiences. The student’s first year is spent 
mastering the basics of science: math, chemistry, and physics. Building on this base, in 
the second year, students begin to take fundamental engineering courses such as statics, 
dynamics, basic circuits and electronics, thermodynamics, and strength of

damental engineering courses such as statics, 
dynamics, basic circuits and electronics, thermodynamics, and strength of materials. By 
the third year, students are taking specialized mechanical engineering courses in the sub-
fields of fluid mechanics, heat transfer, dynamic systems and controls, materials, 
mechanical design, and manufacturing. Finally, during the senior year, students have the 
opportunity to both broaden and deepen their knowledge of the field through technical 
elective courses. At the end of the curriculum, students take the capstone senior design 
course in which the knowledge and skills they have learned are applied to projects 
submitted to the department by industrial firms or by faculty members. Engineering design, 
communication, teamwork, and laboratory experi

epartment by industrial firms or by faculty members. Engineering design, 
communication, teamwork, and laboratory experiences are integrated throughout the 
curriculum from the first year to the last year. 
During their junior and senior years students are encouraged to take courses and involve 
themselves in research projects in cross-disciplinary areas with Civil and Environmental 
Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, and others.

To be eligible to enroll in the ME courses listed in the third year of the curriculum, a student 
must have completed, with a combined 2.25 grade point average, the mathematics, 
physics, computer science, and electrical and computer engineering courses listed in the 
first two years.

Students should have a satisfactory English Proficiency

or 400 level UIUC credit courses.  
ii). Transfer Requirement

other exams approved by UIUC, and maintain a good standing on academic studies that 
all term GPAs, overall Technical GPA on UIUC courses are suggested to be above 2.5. In 
junior year, students will apply for international transfer to be admitted to UIUC and change 
academic status from non-degree student to degree student in Fall semester of senior year.

Electrical Engineering and Automation 
Curriculum 
(Updated in May 2023) 
 
1. Program Educational Objectives  
Electrical engineering and Automation is a multifaceted discipline that over the last century 
has produced an astounding progression of technological innovations that have shaped 
virtually every aspect of modern life. Electrical engineers need a broad and solid foundation in 
mathematics and physics to support their education in the engineering principles of analysis, 
synthesis, design, implementation, and testing of the devices and systems that provide the 
bedrock of modern energy, communication, sensing, computing, medical, security, and 
defense infrastructures. Within each sub-discipline one can find application domains that 
strongly rely on hands-on experimental wo
source: Electrical Engineering and Automation (College of Electrical Engineering).pdf

text:
astructures. Within each sub-discipline one can find application domains that 
strongly rely on hands-on experimental work or that are based on theoretical, mathematical, 
and computational approaches. The multidisciplinary nature of electrical engineering 
education addresses the growing demand for the innovation and design of sensing, 
communication, computing, and decision-making systems of increasing complexity in 
consumer, defense, and medical applications. 
2. Graduation Requirement 
1) 
Curriculum Requirement 
The curriculum leading to the degree of Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering from 
ZJU requires 135 credits and is organized into required courses; technical electives; liberal 
education; and other electives.

power and energy systems.

text:
Electronic Science and Technology, ISEE 
 
COLLEGE OF INFORMATION SCIENCE AND ELECTRONIC ENGINEERING  
(ISEE) 
Electronic Science and Technology Curriculum (2020) 
 
(Updated in May 2023) 
 
1. Program Educational Objectives  
Electronic Science and Technology focuses on the design and application of large and small scale 
electronic systems to process and transmit energy and information in this professionally 
accredited degree. This program has a balanced core and a flexible elective structure designed to 
equip our graduates in the areas of electromagnetics, electronics and microelectronics, integrated 
circuits, digital signal processing and photonics, etc. We expect our graduates to progress to 
positions in industry or graduate study, or to carry the attributes of an engineering educ
source: Electronic Science and Technology (College of Information Science and Electronic Engineering) 废.pdf

text:
our graduates to progress to 
positions in industry or graduate study, or to carry the attributes of an engineering education into 
other disciplines. These programs of study must include mathematics and basic sciences, 
fundamentals and applications in several engineering sciences, and team-based experience in the 
process of design, where theory is applied in the context of real-world needs and constraints, and 
where judgement must be exercised. Our graduates should be able to think critically in solving 
problems and managing tasks and communicates effectively in multidisciplinary professional 
environments. To be a responsible member of the engineering profession, each graduate must be 
aware of social, ethical, environmental and economic factors and constraints on engineering 
activi
source: Electronic Science and Technology (College of Information Science and Electronic Engineering) 废.pdf

text:
ch graduate must be 
aware of social, ethical, environmental and economic factors and constraints on engineering 
activity, and must understand the importance of these matters in a global context. We aspire to 
have our graduates exhibit intellectual depth and creativity, uphold high ethical standards, and 
show a commitment to the betterment of society through service and professional work.  
2. Graduation Requirement  
The curriculum leading to the degree of Bachelor of Electronic Science and Technology from 
ZJU requires 135 credits and is organized into required courses, technical electives, liberal 
education, Chinese language and society study for international students, and other electives.
source: Electronic Science and Technology (College of Information Science and Electronic Engineering) 废.pdf

text:
Zhejiang University-University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign Institute 
(ZJU-UIUC INSTITUTE) 
Electronics and Computer Engineering 
Curriculum 
(Updated in August 2025) 
1. Program Educational Objectives 
Electronics and Computer engineering focuses on the development of vital computing 
technologies, ranging from chips to computers to networks to programming tools to key 
algorithms for building exciting applications. Fundamentally, computer engineering addresses 
the problem of building scalable, trustworthy computing systems, and the faculty's interests 
span a broad spectrum of issues pertinent to this theme. Computer engineering has taken the 
lead in revolutionizing many science and engineering disciplines with parallel computing, from 
chips to clouds to planet-scale critical infrast
source: Electronics and Computer Engineering  (ZJU-UIUC Institute) 2025-08 updates.pdf

text:
many science and engineering disciplines with parallel computing, from 
chips to clouds to planet-scale critical infrastructures, and has defined new standards of 
security, privacy, and dependability for systems ranging from small circuits to the electric 
power grids of many nations. Students need a broad and sound set of mathematical and 
computing skills and are well-served by a flexible curriculum that enables them to pursue topics 
of interest among the many sub-disciplines in computing. 
2. Graduation Requirement 
The curriculum leading to the Bachelor of Engineering from ZJUI requires 137 hours and is 
organized into required courses and elective courses.
source: Electronics and Computer Engineering  (ZJU-UIUC Institute) 2025-08 updates.pdf

text:
English Proficiency Tests for Degree Transfer to UIUC
https://www.admissions.illinois.edu/policies#:~:text=The%20majority%20of%20admitted%20students,the%20minimum%20total%20is%207.5.
Transfer Applicants
Transfer applicants must meet one of the following conditions to satisfy our minimum English proficiency requirement. These are only the minimum requirements needed to meet English proficiency. In many cases, the standards needed to gain admission will be much higher, so it would be to the student's advantage to submit all test scores demonstrating a high level of English competency. Test scores that will be used to fulfill the English proficiency requirement must be taken within two years of the student's date of enrollment in the university.
TOEFL (Internet-based Test of English as a Fore
source: English Proficiency Tests for Degree Transfer to UIUC.docx

text:
within two years of the student's date of enrollment in the university.
TOEFL (Internet-based Test of English as a Foreign Language or the TOEFL iBT Home Edition*)
Exams taken on or after January 21, 2026: Score a minimum of 5.0 total with subscores all 4.5+
Exams taken before January 21, 2026: Score a minimum of 100 total with subscores all 20+
IELTS (Academic International English Testing System)
Score a minimum of 7.5 total with subscores all 6.5+
Duolingo English Test
Score a minimum of 130 overall with subscores 125+ (Comprehension, Conversation, Literacy, Production)
Complete all secondary (years 9 to 12) and post-secondary schooling in an approved country† where English is the official language.
Score high enough on the English section of the ACT or Evidence-Based Reading and Writi
source: English Proficiency Tests for Degree Transfer to UIUC.docx

text:
nglish is the official language.
Score high enough on the English section of the ACT or Evidence-Based Reading and Writing section of the SAT. We'll review these scores on an individual basis in lieu of TOEFL iBT, IELTS, or Duolingo English Test scores to determine if they meet English proficiency. We encourage students to take the TOEFL iBT, IELTS, or Duolingo English Test if they're concerned their ACT or SAT scores might not be high enough to prove English proficiency for admission.
After graduating from high school, complete two years of continuous full-time study at a regionally accredited college or university in the U.S. or a recognized college or university in an approved country† where English is the official language. Full-time study is defined as at least 12 semester hours per s
source: English Proficiency Tests for Degree Transfer to UIUC.docx

text:
approved country† where English is the official language. Full-time study is defined as at least 12 semester hours per semester or 12 quarter hours per quarter of transferable coursework, excluding: English as a second language (ESL) courses; developmental coursework related to instruction in English reading, composition, and/or grammar; test credit; and failed courses. Attendance at an English language institute can't be used to satisfy this requirement. Coursework must be completed within two years of the proposed date of enrollment. Coursework completed prior to high school graduation date may not be used to meet this requirement.
*The TOEFL iBT Special Home Edition will be accepted for spring and fall 2026, but a decision has not been made for future terms.
†Approved countries include
source: English Proficiency Tests for Degree Transfer to UIUC.docx

text:
ll be accepted for spring and fall 2026, but a decision has not been made for future terms.
†Approved countries include Australia, the Bahamas, Barbados, Belize, Bermuda, Canada (excluding Quebec), the Cayman Islands, Dominica, Gambia, Ghana, Grenada, Guyana, Ireland, Jamaica, Kenya, Lesotho, Malawi, New Zealand, Nigeria, Saint Kitts, Sierra Leone, South Africa, Trinidad and Tobago, the United Kingdom, and the United States (excluding Puerto Rico).
source: English Proficiency Tests for Degree Transfer to UIUC.docx

text:
Information Engineering, ISEE
COLLEGE OF INFORMATION SCIENCE AND ELECTRONIC ENGINEERING
(ISEE)
Information Engineering Curriculum
(Updated in January 2025)
1. Educational Objectives
Information Engineering encompasses telecommunications, networking, signal and information
processing - all important and rapidly growing industries in today's information age. It provides
students with an understanding of the entire stack of modern networked computers, from the
design and architecture of the CPU in a smartphone, through the information theory and
wireless protocols connecting it to the internet, to the operating systems and databases
providing back-end support in the cloud.
2.
Graduation Requirement
The curriculum leading to the degree of Bachelor of Information Engineering at ZJU requires
135

.

ST 4001  
Senior Thesis  
at  
ZJU-UIUC Institute  
AY 2025~2026

10 Funding management ......................................................................................... 15 
 
 
 
 
Appendix list  
 
Appendix I - The timetable of senior thesis (ST 4001) in AY 2025 ~ 2026 
Appendix II - Nomination Form for Senior Thesis Projects 
Appendix III - Task plan of Thesis Project 
Appendix IV - Template of Thesis Proposal 
Appendix V - Midterm Self-check Form for Senior Thesis 
Appendix VI - Template of Senior Thesis 
Appendix VII - Academic Integrity Commitment of Senior Thesis 
Appendix VIII - On-site minutes of oral defense for senior thesis 
Appendix IX - Assessment Form of Senior Thesis 
Appendix X - ZJUI Compact on Confidential Control of Senior Thesis 
 
 
Note: Please refer to the file in the attachment to find appendix.

3 
 
1 General Information  
1.1 General Aims 
Senior Theses (as theses) is an important component of talent development in ZJU-
UIUC institute (as ZJUI) and Zhejiang university. It serves as a crucial practical 
teaching segment in undergraduate education and reflects the quality of college 
education at Zhejiang University. Its aim is to cultivate students' ability to 
comprehensively apply the foundational theories, professional knowledge, and basic 
skills learned during their undergraduate studies to analyze and solve real 
engineering problems. This has a profound impact on the growth of undergraduate 
students and their adaptability to societal needs. To effectively serve, support and 
ensure this aim, to further strengthen the standards and quality management of 
these, and to cont

y serve, support and 
ensure this aim, to further strengthen the standards and quality management of 
these, and to continuously improve their quality, ZJUI publishes this document 
according to the "Implementation on Undergraduate Thesis (Design Projects) at 
Zhejiang University" (ZJU Policy [2018] No. 3) and other regulations of Zhejiang 
University regarding theses and in consideration of the practical circumstances at 
ZJUI. 
1.2 General Requirements  
Senior theses require ZJUI undergraduate students to independently complete a 
designated senior thesis project under the guidance of a supervisor and write a thesis 
that meets a series of specified requirements.  
In terms of knowledge requirements, students should integrate knowledge and skills 
from multiple disciplines to analyze an

terms of knowledge requirements, students should integrate knowledge and skills 
from multiple disciplines to analyze and solve engineering problems, deepening 
theoretical understanding, expanding knowledge areas, and extending professional 
skills. 
Regarding capability development, students should learn to research, collect, and 
analyze and organization of scientific literature, correctly cite publications and use 
reference books, and master fundamental methods for scientific research, and 
improve the skills of technical writing. They should also master basic experimental 
methods to enhance their capability to analyze and solve engineering problems in the 
real world. 
In terms of comprehensive quality development, students should cultivate a serious 
scientific attitude and establi

ld. 
In terms of comprehensive quality development, students should cultivate a serious 
scientific attitude and establish correct engineering, economic, and holistic 
perspectives. 
Senior thesis is handled as an undergraduate course, course code ST 4001, at ZJUI. 
Students will acquire 2 ZJU academic credits upon satisfying all the requirements of 
senior theses and passing the course.  
1.3 Timetable 
Senior theses as a UG course, ST 4001, starts in October and completes in mid-June 
of each academic year. The detail segments and key milestone due dates of

4 
 
continuous assessments and thesis submission are listed in Appendix I. 
 
2 Supervisor Qualification  
Supervisors should hold high professional and ethnic standards and focus on the 
development of students' independent research abilities, problem-solving skills, and 
innovative capabilities in engineering design and basic scientific research. 
Supervisors should emphasize inspiring students’ creativity, encouraging their 
initiative, and enthusiasm in research and design projects. The supervisors should 
match one of the following criteria:  
1) A supervisor should possess a high level of professional competence and hold 
a faculty position with at least an intermediate title or equivalent academic 
rank (including lecturer) at ZJUI. 
2) Co-supervisors should possess a high level of

diate title or equivalent academic 
rank (including lecturer) at ZJUI. 
2) Co-supervisors should possess a high level of professional competence, and 
hold an academic or research position with at least an intermediate title or 
equivalent academic rank at Zhejiang University, other prestigious academic 
institutes, research organization, or industrial corporates that are endorsed by 
Zhejiang University.  
3) The supervisor qualification of undergraduate theses, including ZJUI faculties 
and those outside ZJUI, will be reviewed and approved by Undergraduate 
Education Committee (refer as UEC) at ZJUI, and archived by UA office. 
Supervisors from industrial partners should be reviewed and renewed every 
three years.  
 
3 Project Assignment 
Projects for senior theses should closely align

d be reviewed and renewed every 
three years.  
 
3 Project Assignment 
Projects for senior theses should closely align with industrial needs in real world, be 
oriented towards emerging frontiers of engineering and society, focus on addressing 
urgent problems faced by industrials and/or enterprises, and emphasize on 
enhancing students' comprehensive abilities to analyze and solve problems. 
Interdisciplinary and innovative topics are highly recommended. 
3.1 Proposing Thesis Projects  
There are several types of senior thesis projects: 
1) Research or design projects proposed by supervisors that are within or inter-
disciplined of the supervisors’ research fields.  
Projects should not have more than 1/3 overlap with those of the preceding year 
from the same supervisor. Faculty should

ojects should not have more than 1/3 overlap with those of the preceding year 
from the same supervisor. Faculty should also avoid the topics that are obsolete 
and/or of little interests or applications in the current industrial fields.  
2) Research or design projects proposed by students that are based upon the

5 
 
proceeding undergraduate research projects of the students.  
If the student has completed a SRTP project of national, province or university 
level or equivalent undergraduate research projects, the student is highly 
recommended to base senior thesis upon these UG research projects and select 
the instructor of UG research projects as thesis supervisor. If the supervisor is not 
a ZJUI faculty, but a ZJU faculty, the project must have a ZJUI faculty as a leading 
supervisor and ZJU faculty can take the role as co-supervisor. 
3) Industrial projects that are appointed by industrial partners of Zhejiang University. 
The project must have a ZJUI faculty as institute supervisor and main supervisor. 
3.2 Project Assignment 
3.2.1  Mutual Selection 
In general, it is a mutual selection pr

upervisor and main supervisor. 
3.2 Project Assignment 
3.2.1  Mutual Selection 
In general, it is a mutual selection process for both students and supervisors to finalize 
the topic and proposal of the project for senior thesis. In principle, only one student 
will be assigned to each project. If the project will be taken by a team, each student 
in the team should have clear workload, different topic focus and outcome in the 
project. A detailed description of the work scope and workload should be submitted 
in advance. 
The mutual selection process of project assignment is as follows: 
1) Supervisors will submit thesis projects with a title and a brief description to 
PeopleSoft system before Oct 17th 2025. 
2) All the projects will be reviewed and approved by UEC at ZJUI. UA office wil

PeopleSoft system before Oct 17th 2025. 
2) All the projects will be reviewed and approved by UEC at ZJUI. UA office will 
post the projects to the course of “ST 4001” in PeopleSoft system. 
3) Student shall log into PS system and select the three projects with priority 
rank for ST 4001. 
4) Supervisor will assign the project to the students s/he selects.  
5) If a student failed to allocate to a thesis project, UA office will inform the 
student to join the second round of project selection.  
6) To ensure the quality of senior theses, each supervisor can instruct no more 
than six (6) thesis projects in one academic year. 
7) Once all the students are allocated with thesis projects, both the student and 
supervisor will be notified of the project assignment by UA office as a kick-off 
f

esis projects, both the student and 
supervisor will be notified of the project assignment by UA office as a kick-off 
for senior thesis by Oct 24th 2025. 
3.2.2  Pre-Assignment of thesis projects 
Alternatively, a thesis project can be assigned to a student via a pre-assign channel. 
The pre-assign process of a thesis project is as follows: 
1) After discussion between a student and a supervisor, a pre-assign project 
should be submitted to the institute by the supervisor. The supervisor shall fill 
up a document (Appendix II: Nomination Form for Senior Thesis Project) with

6 
 
a title, an abstract (no more than 100 words) of the project and student’s 
information. 
2) The pre-assign projects will be reviewed and approved by UEC at ZJUI.  
3) UA office will assign the project to the student in PeopleSoft system.  
4) Once all the students are allocated with thesis projects, both the student and 
supervisor will be notified of the project assignment by UA office as a kick-off 
for senior thesis. 
5) If a student wants to take on a thesis project with supervisors outside ZJUI 
institute, the project shall be submitted via pre-assign channel. The student 
shall find a ZJUI faculty as main supervisor and the supervisors outside ZJUI 
institute shall be a co-supervisor. The project shall be submitted by ZJUI 
supervisor. 
6) Pre-assign project with co-supervisor

shall be a co-supervisor. The project shall be submitted by ZJUI 
supervisor. 
6) Pre-assign project with co-supervisor outside ZJUI Institute may not be 
counted into the six (6) thesis projects of the ZJUI supervisor. 
 
4 Thesis proposal 
4.1 Task plan of thesis project 
A student should meet her/his supervisor within one (1) week after the project is 
assigned and establish the details of the project according to Appendix III: Task plan 
of thesis project (refer as “task”).  
4.2 Requirements of thesis proposal  
A student should work on a thesis proposal under the instruction of his/her supervisor 
and write a thesis proposal according to the requirements and the template 
(Appendix IV) of ZJUI institute. The thesis proposal should include the following parts: 
1) A thorough literatur

plate 
(Appendix IV) of ZJUI institute. The thesis proposal should include the following parts: 
1) A thorough literature review and/or survey of industrial/markets to introduce 
the current frontier situation and obstacles in the field and to indicate the 
novelty and significance of the projects. 
2) The aims of the project and a detailed elaboration on the approaches/design 
to achieve the aims with sufficient justifications and validations. 
3) The time plan and the expected outcome of the project if it is successfully 
carried out. 
4) Preliminary results and/or current progress of the projects. 
5) At least 10 references with no more than 3 citations of websites and no more than 
3 citations in Chinese. 
4.3 Submission of the proposal document 
Students should write a proposal of the

no more than 
3 citations in Chinese. 
4.3 Submission of the proposal document 
Students should write a proposal of the thesis project under the guidance of her/his 
supervisor. After the proposal has been approved by the supervisor, the student 
should obtain supervisor’s signature and submit the thesis proposal as one pdf file to

7 
 
course blackboard website before Jan 23rd, 2026. Thesis proposal submitted on time 
will receive 10 points. 
 
5 Mid-term check-up 
Students will fill up Appendix V: Midterm Self-check Form for Senior Thesis, under 
the guidance of the supervisor(s) and submit the document to course blackboard 
website before March 27th, 2026. Students will receive 5 points if a mid-term self 
check form with supervisor signature is submitted to institute on time. ZJUI will 
organize mid-term check-up on all the thesis projects in the 7th week of Spring 
semester in the academic year. 
According to the self-check form, supervisors should check if the progress of the 
project is satisfactory, including but not limits to the following aspects, the selection 
of project, and the management and project pr

factory, including but not limits to the following aspects, the selection 
of project, and the management and project progress according to the schedule in 
“task” document. 
UA office will conduct random check on the “midterm self-check form for senior 
thesis” and inform the student and supervisor if a necessary amendment is required. 
Supervisors should also provide the guidance and comments on project amendments 
if problems are discovered during mid-term check-up. 
 
6 Senior Theses and Oral Defense 
6.1 Senior thesis 
6.1.1  Every student should write a senior thesis under the guidance of her/his 
supervisor(s). The main content of senior thesis, excluding references, should be at 
least twenty (20) pages by following the institute template (Appendix VI: Template 
of ZJUI Senior Thes

s, should be at 
least twenty (20) pages by following the institute template (Appendix VI: Template 
of ZJUI Senior Thesis). A senior thesis should include the following parts, but not 
limited to: 
- A title, and an abstract of the project followed by an outline of the written 
thesis. 
- A chapter of literature review and project introduction. 
- Chapters of research work, such as experiments, programming, design figures, 
tests, theoretical reasoning and/or deduction, etc. 
- chapters of detail analysis of the research outcome, such as experimental 
results, software and the testing outcome, design products and testing 
outcome, etc. 
- at least 10 references. Chinese literature should be no more than 3. Website 
citations should be no more than 3. 
- specifications for different types

literature should be no more than 3. Website 
citations should be no more than 3. 
- specifications for different types of projects: 
a) For research projects, the thesis should include 1) a thorough literature review, 
2) substantial research experiments, 2) detail data analysis, 3) sufficient references

8 
 
and 5) project conclusion. 
b) For engineering design projects, the thesis should include thorough 1) 
literature review, 2) detail design figures, 3) photo and video of the demonstrations 
on the design products, 4) test methods, results report and analysis of the product, 
and 5) project conclusion. 
c) For software developing projects, the thesis should include thorough 1) 
literature review, 2) software vendor list, 3) description and manual book of the 
software, 4) the analytic report of software testing and 5) project conclusion. 
6.1.2  Submission of Senior Thesis 
a) A student should sign the document of “Academic Integrity Commitment of 
Senior Thesis “(refer as commitment, Appendix VII) and strictly hold academic 
integrity, match professional standards, respect and protect

er as commitment, Appendix VII) and strictly hold academic 
integrity, match professional standards, respect and protect the legal rights of 
intellectual properties. Any misconducts and/or violations to academic integrity in 
senior thesis will be reported to ZJUI UA office and be handled by following the 
management policy of student misconducts at ZJU-UIUC institute. 
b) An electronic copy of thesis draft should be submitted to course blackboard 
website as one pdf file before May 22nd, 2026. Appendix IX: Assessment Form of 
Senior Thesis (refer as “assess form”) with supervisor’s comments and signature 
should be attached to the draft. Committee will review the thesis draft and decide if 
the student can enter oral defense.  
c) After necessary revision according to Committee comments

draft and decide if 
the student can enter oral defense.  
c) After necessary revision according to Committee comments from oral 
defense, the final version of senior thesis should be printed double faces in A4 
pages and be bundled with other documents according to the checklist in thesis 
template (Appendix VI). The printed bundle of senior thesis and relevant 
documents should be submitted to UA office before June 12th 2026.  
d) An electronic copy of the final version of senior thesis should be submitted to 
course blackboard website as one pdf file before June 12th 2026. The electronic 
copy of senior thesis should be consistent with the printed copy. 
6.2 Oral defense 
6.2.1  Oral defense will be organized by UA office at ZJUI on June 4th, 2026. 
6.2.2  Students and supervisors shou

nse 
6.2.1  Oral defense will be organized by UA office at ZJUI on June 4th, 2026. 
6.2.2  Students and supervisors should attend oral defense in person at the time and 
location designated by UA office.  
 6.2.3  Students should prepare PowerPoint slides for oral presentation of thesis 
project to elaborate the project background, aims, experimental/design approaches, 
analysis of results/outcome/products and conclusion. 
6.2.4  Oral defense of each student will take roughly 20 minutes, including 10 
minutes of oral presentation, 5~10 minutes of Q&A. 
6.2.5 The secretary of oral defense should record the minutes of oral presentation and 
Q&A sections in the document of Appendix VIII: On-site minutes of oral defense.

9 
 
6.2.6  Oral defense will be assessed by thesis committee according to the following 
criteria in section 6.3. The comments and scores should be recorded in Appendix IX: 
Assess Form.  
6.3 Assessment of Senior Thesis 
6.3.1 
 The thesis draft should be reviewed and approved by project supervisor. 
Thesis supervisor(s) should make a consensus decision if the thesis draft is ready for 
submission and fill up the comments to Appendix IX: Assess Form.  
6.3.2  Institute will organize thesis committees according to the research fields and 
disciplines of the thesis projects. Each committee is formed by 3~5 faculties with one 
chairman. Each committee will review and evaluate around 15 senior theses. In 
principle, the supervisor of the thesis should be assigned to the thesis committee that

ate around 15 senior theses. In 
principle, the supervisor of the thesis should be assigned to the thesis committee that 
will evaluate the thesis. 
6.3.3  The committee will assess the thesis draft and decide if the student can enter 
the oral defense. A thesis draft will be cross reviewed by at least two committee 
members according to the marking rubrics. A consensus grade will be filled up in the 
assessment form of Appendix IX: Assessment Form of Senior Thesis.  
Note:  
- 
If Committee decides that the student cannot enter oral defense, committee 
chair should submit Appendix IX: Assess form to UA office before June 3rd 2026.  
6.3.4  The components are considered when committee assesses a thesis project: 
-  
The overall quality of written thesis, including but not limited to the pr

hen committee assesses a thesis project: 
-  
The overall quality of written thesis, including but not limited to the project 
selection, workload, completeness, plagiarism level, advance composition, 
novelty and so on. 
-  
the quality of the PowerPoint slides of oral defense.  
-  
performance in oral defense including demonstration of project and Q&A. 
-       working performance during the entire project. 
-       academic discipline and attendance.

6.3.6  ZJUI encourages students to apply for patents and publications based upon 
the outcome of thesis proj

6.3.6  ZJUI encourages students to apply for patents and publications based upon 
the outcome of thesis projects. If patents and/or publications are accepted by 
scientific journals or prevailing conference, “outcome scores” will be awarded

11 
 
accordingly.  
NOTE: the scores can ONLY be awarded when a thesis project yields qualified 
outcome. 
6.3.7  After oral defense, the chairman of thesis committee will collect the 
comments and scores from all the committee members and draft a consensus 
comment. The chairman will fill up the comments and grades in assess form 
(Appendix IX) and sign the document. The assess form should be submitted to UA 
office with 3 days after oral defense. 
6.3.8  If a student receives a “fail” grade in senior thesis, the student will get a “F” in 
ST 4001 and should not proceed to graduation. The student should retake ST 4001 in 
the next academic year.  
 
6.4 Plagiarism checks of senior thesis 
ZJUI will be responsible for organizing and implementing the plagiarism check of 
Senior theses acco

s of senior thesis 
ZJUI will be responsible for organizing and implementing the plagiarism check of 
Senior theses according to the requirements, generating a plagiarism check report to 
assist in academic integrity management. The plagiarism check uses technical means 
to conduct an initial screening of the thesis quality. The plagiarism check report and 
the plagiarism rate are the main basis to determine whether the thesis meets the 
standards for submission. The content to be checked includes abstracts, main 
content, and references. Senior thesis will be processed according to plagiarism 
report and rate as follows: 
1. 
Plagiarism rate below 10% is considered passed, with appropriate modifications 
based on the check report; 
2. Plagiarism rate between 10%-20% is considered passed a

d, with appropriate modifications 
based on the check report; 
2. Plagiarism rate between 10%-20% is considered passed after modification and 
the student can proceed to oral defense upon further review by thesis committee; 
3. 
Plagiarism rate above 20% is considered not passed, requiring major 
modifications and a second plagiarism check; 
4. If the plagiarism rate does not meet the requirements, student can file an appeal 
with a written explanation approved by supervisor. Committee will review the 
appeal document and decide if the thesis can proceed to the thesis review. 
5. 
Theses that do not pass the second plagiarism check cannot proceed to the 
following steps of thesis review and oral defense.

f thesis review and oral defense. 
 
7 Requirements on the students in senior thesis 
7.1 Kick-off the thesis project 
7.1.1  A student is encouraged to meet potential supervisors and discuss the project 
before applying for the project, especially in the case that the student would like to 
propose a project by himself/herself.

12 
 
7.1.2  A student should log into PeopleSoft system to select and rank three projects 
before October 24th, 2025. 
7.1.3  A student should meet all the supervisors of his/her project within one (1) 
week after the project is assigned by UA office. 
7.1.4  Student will fill up “task” document with his/her supervisor during the kick-off 
meeting. Students should work according to the plan and time schedule listed in the 
document. At the end of the project, students should bundle the document to the 
thesis and submit the document to institute along with other thesis documents. 
7.1.5 
Student should write a project proposal according to the requirements in 
section 4 and submit the proposal to course blackboard website by Jan 23rd, 2026. 
7.2 During the process of the thesis project 
7

and submit the proposal to course blackboard website by Jan 23rd, 2026. 
7.2 During the process of the thesis project 
7.2.1 Student should meet the supervisor and co-supervisor if applicable, on regular 
basis and at least 0.5 hour per week to report and discuss the project progress.  
7.2.2 
Student should record daily work activities and outcome of the experiments, 
programming, tests, analysis of the results, etc. on lab notebook.  
7.2.3 
A student should follow the instruction and guidance of the supervisor and 
complete the research work and documents of thesis project on due course.   
7.2.4 
A student should attend all the thesis activities. If a student would be absent 
to thesis activities, the student needs to seek leave approval from both supervisor and 
institute via online a

absent 
to thesis activities, the student needs to seek leave approval from both supervisor and 
institute via online application. If a student is absence without leave approval more 
than 1/3 of the project’s duration, the student is not allowed to enter thesis defense 
and course grade will be recorded as “0” mark. 
7.2.5 
A student should complete all the oral and written reports of senior thesis 
under the instruction of project supervisor(s). 
7.2.6 
A student should participate in the mid-term check-up of thesis projects. A 
student should discuss the project progress and fill up the “midterm self-check form 
for senior thesis” (Appendix V) together with her/his supervisor(s) and submit the 
form to UA office for random check by March 27th, 2026. 
7.3 Requirements for senior theses

pervisor(s) and submit the 
form to UA office for random check by March 27th, 2026. 
7.3 Requirements for senior theses and oral defense  
7.3.1  Students should write a thesis under the guidance of her/his supervisor(s). The 
thesis should be written by following the instructions in section 6.1 and institute 
template (Appendix VI).  
7.3.2 
Students should submit an electronic copy of thesis draft according to section 
6 before May 22nd 2026. 
7.3.3 
Upon approval by the thesis committee, students should attend the oral 
defense of senior thesis and satisfied with the requirements of oral defense listed in

13 
 
section 6.2. 
7.3.4 
After oral defense, students should prepare the thesis bundle book containing 
all the documents related to thesis and an electronic copy of thesis book by following 
the instruction in section 9. The printed book should be submitted to the thesis book 
to the institute office and the electronic copy should be uploaded to course 
blackboard website before June 12th, 2026. 
 
8 Requirements For the Supervisors 
8.1 Propose thesis projects 
8.1.1 
Supervisors should propose projects closely aligning with industrial needs in 
the real world, be oriented towards emerging frontiers of engineering and society, 
focus on addressing urgent problems faced by enterprises, and emphasize on 
enhancing students' comprehensive abilities to analyze and solve problems. 
Interdis

d by enterprises, and emphasize on 
enhancing students' comprehensive abilities to analyze and solve problems. 
Interdisciplinary and innovative topics are highly recommended.  
8.1.2 Supervisors should not reuse more than 1/3 of the projects she/he supervised 
in the preceding years.  
8.1.3 
Supervisors should avoid the projects that are obsoleted and/or of little 
interests or applications in the current industrial fields.   
8.1.4 Supervisors cannot take on more than six (6) projects in one academic year. 
8.2 Project proposal 
8.2.1 Supervisors should meet the students within one (1) week the project 
assignment is announced by UA office. 
8.2.2 During the meeting, supervisor should fill up “task” document with the 
student that includes the details of the requirements for the project

upervisor should fill up “task” document with the 
student that includes the details of the requirements for the project and the time 
table of the project.  
8.2.3 If the project has a co-supervisor outside ZJUI, the ZJUI supervisor should 
liaise all the co-supervisors to fill up the “task” document with the student and 
arrange the student to meet all the co-supervisors within one (1) week after the 
project assignment is announced by UA office.  
8.2.4 Supervisors should instruct and guide the student to conduct a thorough 
literature review on the research field of the project and to write a project proposal. 
8.2.5 Supervisors will be assigned to one or a few theses committees according to 
the major and research fields of the projects.

14 
 
8.3 During the progress of thesis projects 
8.3.1 
Supervisor should meet the student at least once a week for at least half an 
hour to check the lab notebook and discuss the progress of theses project. 
8.3.2 Supervisor should provide sufficient instruction and guidance to lead the 
student to complete the theses project on time. 
8.3.3 
Supervisor should ensure the students submit all the written reports and 
attend oral assessments on time. 
8.4 Requirements for midterm evaluation 
8.4.1 Supervisor should review the project progress with students and provide the 
instructions on the amendments of the project if any problem is discovered.  
8.4.2 Supervisor should instruct the student to fill up the document of “midterm 
self-check form” (Appendix V) and urge the student to submit

d instruct the student to fill up the document of “midterm 
self-check form” (Appendix V) and urge the student to submit the form to course 
blackboard website on time. 
8.5 Requirements for theses and oral defense 
8.5.1 
Supervisors should instruct the student to write a senior thesis according to 
the institute requirements listed in section 6.1. Supervisor should review the senior 
thesis of his/her mentee and provide sufficient comments and suggestions for 
revisions. 
8.5.2 Supervisor should assess her/his mentee’s thesis by following the marking 
rubrics in section 6.3.4 that requires a thorough review on the quality of the thesis, 
including the completion level, outcome quality, the work capability, and attitudes of 
the students, etc. Supervisor should provide comments and sign A

outcome quality, the work capability, and attitudes of 
the students, etc. Supervisor should provide comments and sign Assess Form 
(Appendix IX). The student will submit the form along with thesis draft for committee 
to review and to decide if the student can proceed to oral defense.  
8.5.3 
Supervisors as a committee member or chairman should attend the oral 
defense of the students assigned by institute and evaluate the students’ oral defenses 
according to the marking rubrics in section 6.3. If a supervisor serves as a committee 
chairman, she/he should consolidate the grades and comments on both the theses 
and oral defense of the student from all the committee members to provide a 
consensus grade and a written comment in Assess Form (Appendix IX).  
9 Archive and confidential cont

bers to provide a 
consensus grade and a written comment in Assess Form (Appendix IX).  
9 Archive and confidential control of senior thesis 
9.1 Thesis Archive 
Students should submit all the documents, products and materials that are related to 
senior thesis to ZJUI institute for archives. The duration of archives is no shorter than 
five (5) years. Students should submit the following two copies of senior thesis before 
June 12th 2026.

15 
 
9.1.1 
A printed copy  
Students should prepare a printed bundle of senior thesis (refer as thesis book). All 
the thesis documents should be bundled by following the order in the thesis template 
(Appendix VI):  
cover page  → checklist → “commitment”→ acknowledgement → abstract → 
content table (including page numbers) → written thesis (page number starting from 
1) → references → appendix (acceptance letter of publication, cover page if 
applicable, publication full text, patent letter, etc.) → author CV → “task”→ “assess 
form of senior thesis”  → “defense minutes” document.  
Printing format: Content table, written thesis, references and appendix should be 
double faces. The rest part of bundle should be single face.  
9.1.2 An electronic copy  
After oral defense, student shoul

ble faces. The rest part of bundle should be single face.  
9.1.2 An electronic copy  
After oral defense, student should upload an electronic copy of thesis book to course 
blackboard website as a single pdf file. Documents for progress records, such as 
checklist, commitment, task, assess forms of thesis, defense minutes, are not 
required for the electronic copy. 
9.2 Confidential control 
9.2.1 For thesis project are under confidential control, all involved personnel 
including student, supervisor(s), reviewers, and thesis committee, should sign a 
document: Appendix X – ZJUI Compact on Confidential Control of Senior Thesis. 
Compact will be kept by UA office within the confidential duration. 
9.2.2 Thesis book should be stamped with “confidential” at the right up corner on 
the front

he confidential duration. 
9.2.2 Thesis book should be stamped with “confidential” at the right up corner on 
the front page. 
9.2.3 Thesis book should indicate the duration of confidential control on the front 
page. Once confidential control expires, the senior thesis will be handled as a regular 
thesis. 
 
10  Funding management 
10.1. ZJUI institute provides 500 RMB to each thesis project.  
10.2. The funding of each thesis project will be assigned to ZJUI supervisor of the 
project and is the supervisor’s responsibility for proper management.  
10.3. The funding can be used for printing thesis book and other related documents, 
purchasing experimental consumables, paying for testing charges, etc., upon 
supervisor’s approval.

Undergraduate Students Leave & Excused
Absence Application
Dear students,
Before submitting your leave of absence application, please carefully read and
understand the following instructions.
 
1. You are not allowed to take a leave for more than four weeks. If this is
the case, you MUST apply to gap the semester.
2. If You apply for leave shorter than 4 weeks and longer than 14 days,
you MUST seek approval from ZJUI Dean, Professor Hao MA in
advance.
3. Unless you are applying for medical leave or family emergency, the
application MUST be submitted at least THREE business days in
advance.
4. In the occasion of emergency for medical leave, you can apply after
your visit to the clinics.
5. For family emergency, you should inform class tutor before you leave
and apply fo
source: Undergraduate Students Leave & Excused Absence Application.pdf

text:
ly after
your visit to the clinics.
5. For family emergency, you should inform class tutor before you leave
and apply for leave as soon as you come back to campus, if you
cannot apply before you leave the campus.
6. Filling out this online form does NOT guarantee that your application
will be approved and you will have an excused absence.

ZJUI Course Offerings in Spring Semester 2026
All the courses offered in Spring Semester 2026 are categorized into the following
three types: 
 
Courses are also categorized as mandatory (section 3 in your curriculum) and
elective ones (section 4 in your curriculum). 
 
Please read all the following notes before dive into the course table.
1. UIUC courses are those taught by UIUC faculties or co-taught by ZJUI and
UIUC faculties. The academic credits (AU) earned from UIUC courses  are
therefore counted as UIUC credits. Final grades will be recorded in both ZJUI
and UIUC systems.
All UIUC courses are highlighted in blue in the course tables.
2. ZJUI courses  are those taught by ZJUI faculties normally without the
involvement of UIUC faculties. AUs earned from ZJUI co
source: ZJUI Course Offerings in Spring Semester 2026.pdf

text:
courses  are those taught by ZJUI faculties normally without the
involvement of UIUC faculties. AUs earned from ZJUI courses are therefore
counted as ZJUI credits.   Grades of these courses will be recorded in
INTL PeopleSoft system and will be transferred to UIUC after students have
been  successfully admitted by UIUC through international transfer during
their junior year.
3. ZJU courses  are those taught by  ZJU faculties that are used to fulfil the
graduation requirement for the ZJU degree.
1. Mandatory courses include fundamental mathematics and science, technical
cores, and composition. Everyone must complete all mandatory courses.
2. Elective courses  include technical courses, general education (liberal
education) and free electives. Elective courses are optional. Students must
ta
source: ZJUI Course Offerings in Spring Semester 2026.pdf

the categories of ZJUI and UIUC courses.
